data.title: Dole expands Nordic footprint with Greenfood fresh produce acquisition
Sodexo S.A. reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Sodexo S.A. press release (SDXAY): Q3
  • Revenue of EUR 6.2B (+1.3% Y/Y).

Outlook

Following a stronger-than-expected third-quarter performance, while maintaining a prudent view of the external environment, Sodexo now expects for Fiscal 2026:

  • Organic revenue growth between +1.2% and +1.5% (previously +0.5% to +1%)
  • Underlying operating profit margin between 3.2% and 3.4%.

data.title: Yara to buy Gulf Coast ammonia plant for $1.3B
data.description:
Yara International (YARIY) (YRAIF) said Thursday it agreed to acquire the Gulf Coast Ammonia production facility in Texas City, Texas, from GCA Holdings LLC, affiliated with Lotus Infrastructure Partners and MB Energy, for $1.3B.

Yara (YARIY) (YRAIF) will own the ammonia plant with an expected nameplate capacity of 1.3M metric tons/year, with Air Products (APD) supplying the industrial gases as part of a long-term supply agreement.

The plant is in commissioning and anticipated to continue ramping up toward full production and stable operations by year-end 2026, with production targeted at above nameplate capacity, the company said.

Yara (YARIY) (YRAIF) said it sees investing in the U.S. as "highly attractive, reinforcing its long-time presence as a reliable provider of crop nutrition solutions and producer of ammonia."

The acquisition follows ​Yara's (YARIY) (YRAIF) recent decision not to proceed ​with its acquisition of ammonia assets ⁠in the Louisiana Clean Energy ​Complex from Air Products (APD).

data.title: These are the top ten H1 performers in the energy sector
data.description:
As we move into the second half of the year, below is a list of the top 10 energy stocks ranked by their H1 performance. The list includes companies from the Oil and Gas Refining and Marketing and Oil and Gas Equipment and Services industries, spanning various market capitalizations.

The list is led by Kodiak Gas Services, Inc. (KGS), with an impressive H1 performance of 93.58%. National Energy Services Reunited Corp. (NESR) and Calumet, Inc. (CLMT) follow closely behind, both posting gains above 83%. Select Water Solutions, Inc. (WTTR) and Delek US Holdings, Inc. (DK) round out the top five performers.

Among the top performers, several stocks carry Strong Buy quant ratings, including NESR with a rating of 4.91, DK and PBF Energy Inc. (PBF) both at 4.81, and Marathon Petroleum Corporation (MPC) at 4.85. Valero Energy Corporation (VLO) stands out as the largest company on the list with a market cap of $80.01B.

Here is the list:

  1. Kodiak Gas Services, Inc. (KGS), H1 perf: 93.58%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.20)

  2. National Energy Services Reunited Corp. (NESR), H1 perf: 83.91%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.91)

  3. Calumet, Inc. (CLMT), H1 perf: 83.34%, Quant Rating: Hold (2.97)

  4. Select Water Solutions, Inc. (WTTR), H1 perf: 80.70%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.45)

  5. Delek US Holdings, Inc. (DK), H1 perf: 79.16%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.81)

  6. PBF Energy Inc. (PBF), H1 perf: 77.14%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.81)

  7. Par Pacific Holdings, Inc. (PARR), H1 perf: 67.33%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.76)

  8. Valero Energy Corporation (VLO), H1 perf: 65.51%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.46)

  9. Oceaneering International, Inc. (OII), H1 perf: 63.92%, Quant Rating: Hold (2.92)

  10. Marathon Petroleum Corporation (MPC), H1 perf: 62.87%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.85)

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC)

data.title: AI memory, chip stocks dip after SK Hynix, Samsung drag down Kospi
data.description:
Shares of memory and AI-related stocks were again in the red premarket on Thursday after yesterday's sell-off put Asian peers under pressure today.

South Korea’s Kospi Index slumped about 7.89%. South Korean memory chip giant SK Hynix (HXSCL) plummeted nearly 15%, while peer Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) tumbled around 9% on the Korea Exchange. Earlier this week, the two countries pledged billions in investment in South Korea aimed at growing the country's semiconductors and AI space. Today the two provided details. 

SK Hynix said it would invest 100T won (about $64.38B) to build memory chip ‌and packaging plants as part of a South Korean investment drive, which was announced earlier this week, aimed at semiconductors and AI. Meanwhile, Samsung plans to invest a combined 140T won ($90B) in South Korea's central Chungcheong region, expanding production of display panels, batteries, semiconductors, and chip materials.

"Samsung and SK Hynix now make up around half the Kospi’s total weight, up from around just a quarter at the end of last year," Zavier Wong, analyst at eToro, said in a note, according to CNBC. This highlights the companies' huge influence on the country’s benchmark index.

Hong Kong and Chinese tech stocks also saw red. China’s largest contract chipmaker and foundry Semiconductor Manufacturing International tumbled about 10% in Hong Kong, while Hua Hong Grace, another Chinese chip foundry listed in Hong Kong, slumped nearly 13%.

American memory giant Micron Technology (MU) — which competes with Samsung and SK Hynix to provide High-Bandwidth Memory, or HBM chips to Nvidia — fell nearly 3% premarket on Thursday. Western Digital (WDC) and Seagate Technology (STX) each declined around 4%, while Sandisk (SNDK) fell about 3%.

Shares of AI chipmakers Nvidia (NVDA) dipped about 1%, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) fell around 2%. Qualcomm (QCOM) and Broadcom (AVGO) each slipped nearly 1%. Cerebras (CBRS) declined about 3%.

Other AI and networking-related stocks were also largely in the red on Thursday. Corning (GLW), Celestica (CLS), and Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) each fell about 3%, while Lumentum (LITE), Coherent (COHR), Ciena (CIEN), and Arista Networks (ANET) each declined around 2%. Cisco (CSCO) was also in the red.

Shares of Arm (ARM), GlobalFoundries (GFS), Intel (INTC) and Marvell Technology (MRVL) each fell nearly 2%, while Analog Devices (ADI) and Texas Instruments (TXN) each dipped about 1%. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) were also in the red.

Chip equipment makers: ASML (ASML) and KLA (KLAC) each dipped about 1%, while Lam Research (LRCX) was also in the red. However, Applied Materials (AMAT) was largely flat but in the green premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Takeda, Insilico sign AI drug discovery pact worth up to $600M
data.description:
Hong Kong-listed Insilico Medicine on Thursday announced a partnership with Japanese drugmaker Takeda (TAK) to discover, develop, and commercialize drug candidates using artificial intelligence as part of a deal worth up to $600M.

Under the collaboration, Boston, Massachusetts-based Insilico will leverage its Pharma.AI platform to identify drug candidates within Takeda’s (TAK) therapeutic areas.

The Tokyo-based pharma giant will then lead the clinical advancement of molecules that meet its scientific and early-development criteria, owning their exclusive worldwide rights.

In exchange, Insilico will be entitled to roughly $60M in project initiation fees, near-term payments, and milestones. The total value of the deal could reach approximately $600M if payments linked to development and commercial milestones are also considered.

Additionally, there will be tiered royalties on potential sales of products that reach commercialization.

“The partnership also supports Takeda’s transition to an AI-native discovery model, as we integrate automation, robotics, and generative AI to advance high-quality candidates more efficiently,” said Chris Arendt, head of research at Takeda (TAK).

“I am excited to partner with one of the top leaders in the biopharmaceutical industry with massive competence in generative AI,” Insilico CEO and founder Alex Zhavoronkov added.
